spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode. surface (high-contrast); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: computed-value, font-variant-emoji.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Toggle simulated forced-colors" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5861138256494592.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on glyph-substitution-explorer
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: glyph-substitution-explorer covers the happy path of Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode. and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
